---
title: Data types and structures in R and Pyhon
description: |
  **Delivery mode:** Online (distance learning) 
 
  **Start date:** Years 1, 2 and 3 | Term 2 - Block 6 
  
  **Credits:** 10 
site-url: https://www.drps.ed.ac.uk/current/dpt/cxhein11068.htm
image: uoe.jpg
#image-alt: |
categories: [code, data, postgraduate]
open-graph: true
twitter-card: true
# will inherit title, description, image
# to change ANYTHING about card, need to fill in EVERYTHING below
#  title: |
#  description: |
#  image: |
#  image-alt: |
#  defaults to 500 x 500 summary: uncomment lines below for large card
#  image-width: 600 
#  image-height: 314
#  card-style: summary_large_image 
#  image-width: 500
#  image-height: 500
#  card-style: summary
draft: false
---
::: {.profile-image-float-left}
![](.jpg){fig-align="centre"}
:::

## About this course

Learn about the foundations of how data is represented and how to effectively work with the types and structures of data in 2 of the core data science programming languages: Python AND R. Concepts illustrated with examples of data types and structures found in the health and social care context. No prior knowledge is necessary, but helpful to have exposure to at least one of the languages before the course.
 
## Assessment
 
Coding tasks and report in both Python and R. 

## Programming

R and Python
